message:
Major expansion of storage layer and CLI with profile management

- storage.rs: Add 429 lines implementing storage abstraction for session data,
  profile persistence, migration framework, SQLite backend with WAL mode,
  atomic writes with temp file rename, compression support for large entries
- main.rs: Add 438 lines implementing profile management subcommands (create,
  list, apply, delete, export, import), batch operations, interactive prompts,
  progress indicators for long operations
- Sync beads issue tracker

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-12f4","title":"E2E: TUI smoke + interaction logging","description":"## Goal
Validate the TUI end-to-end with scripted interactions and detailed logs.

## Requirements
- Drive a scripted TUI session (pane list, events feed, search, workflow panel) using a deterministic fixture DB.
- Capture session transcript + keypress log + screenshots (or text snapshots) as artifacts.
- Ensure no panics and output remains stable under resize/refresh.

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E TUI scenario passes locally and in CI.
- Failure artifacts include TUI transcript, interaction log, and snapshots.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:20:40.548004359Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T20:43:38.242172171Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-12f4","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:09:58Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-12f4","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:09:58Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-12f4","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:09:58Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-13y1","title":"Config validation + wa notify test","description":"## What
Extend notifications config and add a CLI test command.

## Why
Misconfigured channels should fail fast with actionable errors and users need a safe connectivity check.

## How
- Extend existing [notifications] config with channel definitions
- CLI: `wa notify test --channel <name>` returns structured success/failure
- Reuse NotificationGate for filters and cooldown

## Success Criteria
- Invalid configs produce clear error messages
- Test command validates redaction and endpoint reachability","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilentCanyon","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:08:19.633409931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T09:18:11.609739890Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T09:18:11.609661384Z","close_reason":"Added notifications config validation and new 'wa notify test --channel' with gate/redaction/delivery checks; added tests; ran fmt/check/clippy/test.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-13y1","depends_on_id":"bd-2uyz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:08:54.236688302Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-13y1","depends_on_id":"bd-ugaj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:08:19.645536516Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":209,"issue_id":"bd-13y1","author":"SilentCanyon","text":"Implemented notifications config validation (severity/agent types, webhook name/URL/duplicates) and added  command with gate+redaction+delivery checks plus CLI parse test. Ran cargo fmt/check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -D warnings, cargo test.","created_at":"2026-02-04T09:17:49Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-15ds","title":"[EPIC] Local RPC socket for robot/MCP (auth + caps)","description":"## Background
Robot/MCP APIs are powerful; local tools need a secure and ergonomic way to connect without parsing CLI output.

## Goals
- Provide a local IPC server over Unix domain socket / named pipe
- Authenticate with short-lived tokens and capability scopes
- Keep parity with robot/MCP schemas

## Non-Goals
- Remote network exposure (handled by distributed mode)

## Considerations
- Must be disabled by default
- Requires strict redaction and audit logging

## Success Criteria
- Local client can call core robot APIs over IPC
- Token and scope checks enforced
- Unit + e2e tests for auth and schema parity","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:19:13.180280826Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:19:13.180280826Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-15el","title":"E2E: retention tiers + cleanup","description":"## Scenarios
- Populate DB with mixed severity events
- Run dry-run cleanup and verify counts
- Apply cleanup and verify retention

## Logging
- Capture before/after stats and deletion counts

## Success Criteria
- E2E artifacts show deterministic cleanup behavior","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:11:45.832562357Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:12:12.319347576Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-15el","depends_on_id":"bd-19i4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:11:45.842172480Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-15el","depends_on_id":"bd-270z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:12:12.319317901Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-15el","depends_on_id":"bd-ybyi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:12:10.272995044Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-19i4","title":"[EPIC] Data lifecycle + retention tiers","description":"## Background
Long-running watchers accumulate large datasets. Operators need predictable retention, with important events kept longer and safe cleanup tools.

## Goals
- Tiered retention based on severity/type
- Data volume dashboard with cleanup suggestions
- Safe preview of cleanup actions

## Non-Goals
- External archival storage (handled by export/sync epics)

## Considerations
- Retention rules must be deterministic and auditable
- Cleanup should never delete data without explicit confirmation

## Success Criteria
- Retention tiers apply without regressions
- Dashboard clearly shows size drivers
- Unit + e2e tests cover cleanup safety","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:10:45.269296346Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:10:45.269296346Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"wa-upg.7.6","title":"E2E: prepare→approve→commit succeeds; mismatch commit denied","description":"# Task: E2E prepare/commit approvals

## Goal
Prove prepare/commit approvals work end-to-end and prevent TOCTOU errors.

## Scenario A (happy path)
- Run `wa prepare ...` for a gated action.
- Approve the plan hash.
- Run `wa commit <hash>` and verify success.

## Scenario B (mismatch)
- Prepare a plan.
- Change the underlying inputs so the recomputed plan differs.
- Attempt commit and verify:
  - denied
  - actionable reason (hash mismatch)

## Requirements
- No fixed sleeps; use wait-for/quiescence.
- Artifacts include:
  - prepare JSON
  - approval grant record
  - commit result + step logs

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E demonstrates both correctness and safety properties.

 {"id":"wa-upg.7.7","title":"Docs: safe approvals (prepare/commit mental model + examples)","description":"# Task: Document prepare/commit approvals

## Goal
Make approval flows intuitive and hard to misuse.

## Requirements
- Document:
  - when to use prepare/commit
  - how plan hashes work
  - how approvals expire and why
  - troubleshooting common errors (hash mismatch, expired)

## Acceptance Criteria
- A user can follow docs to safely run a gated action without confusion.

 {"id":"wa-upg.8","title":"[EPIC] Noise control: dedupe/cooldown/mute (clear needs-attention signal)","description":"# [EPIC] Noise control: dedupe/cooldown/mute

## Mission
Make wa's event/notification surfaces **high signal, low noise**.

We want:
- a clear "needs attention" list
- no spam loops
- meaningful escalation when something persists

## Why This Matters
If wa spams, users mute it mentally (or literally), and then real incidents get missed.
Noise control is essential for:
- notification webhooks
- TUI event feed
- CLI status/triage output

## Scope
- Dedupe identical events within a time window.
- Cooldown repeated notifications per (event key, target).
- Muting:
  - user can mute an event type/key for N minutes
  - muted items are still visible in a "muted" view
- Escalation:
  - if an event persists beyond threshold, escalate severity and surface prominently

## Success Criteria
- Repeated detections do not create spam.
- Persistent issues are escalated and remain visible.
- Users can quickly see what needs attention.

## Testing
- Unit tests for dedupe/cooldown/mute logic.
- E2E:
  - create repeated event
  - verify suppression and escalation
  - verify mute behavior

## Acceptance Criteria
- Noise control features exist across CLI/TUI/notifications.
- Tests and E2E validate deterministic behavior with good logs.

-{"id":"wa-upg.8.1","title":"Design: event dedupe keys, cooldown windows, and escalation policy","description":"# Task: Design noise control policy

## Goal
Define the deterministic noise control rules.

## Requirements
- Define event identity key:
  - event type + pane_uuid + rule_id (or equivalent)
  - include relevant parameters (but not raw secrets)
- Define:
  - dedupe window
  - notification cooldown window
  - escalation thresholds (count/age)
- Define how muting works:
  - scope (workspace/global)
  - duration
  - how to list muted items

## Acceptance Criteria
- Policy is explicit and implementable without ad-hoc special cases.

 {"id":"wa-upg.8.2","title":"Implement event dedupe + notification cooldown (high-signal feeds)","description":"# Task: Implement dedupe + cooldown

## Goal
Implement the core noise control mechanisms.

## Requirements
- Event dedupe:
  - collapse repeated identical events within window
  - increment a counter and update "last_seen"
- Notification cooldown:
  - avoid sending the same notification repeatedly
  - include suppressed count in subsequent notifications
- Persistence:
  - store dedupe state in DB (or bounded in-memory with periodic flush)

## Testing
- Unit tests for:
  - dedupe key correctness
  - cooldown behavior
  - counter increments and last_seen updates

## Acceptance Criteria
- Repeated events do not spam logs/notifications while still remaining visible.

 {"id":"wa-upg.8.3","title":"Implement mute/unmute (event key) + listing muted items","description":"# Task: Implement mute/unmute for events

## Goal
Give users explicit control over noisy events without losing visibility.

## Requirements
- Commands:
  - `wa mute <event_key> --for <duration>`
  - `wa unmute <event_key>`
  - `wa muted` (list current mutes)
- Scoping:
  - workspace-scoped by default
  - optional global scope (explicit flag)
- Storage:
  - persist mutes with TTL

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - TTL expiry
  - scope enforcement
- Output tests:
  - JSON schema for list

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users can mute noisy events without hiding critical issues.

 {"id":"wa-upg.8.4","title":"Integrate noise control into status/triage outputs and TUI event feed","description":"# Task: Integrate noise control into UX surfaces

## Goal
Ensure noise control is visible and intuitive in user-facing surfaces.

## Requirements
- CLI:
  - status/triage shows deduped events with counts and last_seen
  - muted events are visible in a separate view
- TUI:
  - event feed shows suppression counts
  - quick actions: mute/unmute
- Notifications:
  - include suppression counts and escalation signals

## Testing
- Output tests for stable formatting.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users can understand what's happening without being spammed.
